name: fixinventory-plugin-gcp

metadata.name: fixinventory-plugin-gcp

documentation:
# fix-plugin-gcp
An GCP collector plugin for Fix.

## License
See [LICENSE](../../LICENSE) for details.

functions.name: test.random_client.build_random_data_client
documentation: This is the random data client discovery function (replaces discovery.build in tests).

functions.name: test.random_client.roundtrip
documentation:
Roundtrip test.
\:param resource_clazz: the resource class to collect
\:param builder: the graph_builder to use
\:param overrides: specific json overrides to specify from request parameters.

> Example: BackupRun= {"name": "instance"}
> Will get the request parameter instance and use it as name for all BackupRun resources.

```
return

the first created resource of the given type
```

classes.name: test.random_client.FixturedClient
documentation:
Forces the `RandomDataClient` to return fixed value(s) for specified field(s) instead of random values.
Example:
```
    fixtures = {"Instance": {"machineType": lambda: "n2-standard-64"}}
    with FixturedClient(random_builder, fixtures):
        GcpInstance.collect_resources(random_builder)
```

functions.name: fix_plugin_gcp.utils.paginate
documentation:
Paginate GCP API list and aggregatedList results.

Args:
    gcp_resource: GCP resource on which we do our paging
    method_name: list method to call. Usually `list` or `aggregatedList`
    items_name: Name of the key in our result that contains the list of items.
        Usually `items`
    subitems_name: When using aggregatedList this contains the actual items.
        Usually the same as the gcp_resource name. E.g. `disks` when requesting
        disks, `instances` when fetching instances, etc.
    exclude_region_resources: Regional resources have their own API and can be
        excluded from aggregatedList calls if so desired

functions.name: fix_plugin_gcp.utils.get_result_data
documentation:
Returns data from a GCP API call result dict.

Args:
    result: Dict containing the result or a GCP API execute() call.
    value: Either directly the name of a key found in result or
        a callable like a lambda that finds the relevant data withing
        result.

functions.name: fix_plugin_gcp.resources.base.parse_json
documentation:
Use this method to parse json into a class. If the json can not be parsed, the error is reported to the core.
Based on configuration, either the exception is raised or None is returned.
\:param json: the json to parse.
\:param clazz: the class to parse into.
\:param builder: the graph builder.
\:param mapping: the optional mapping to apply before parsing.
\:return: The parsed object or None.

methods.name: fix_plugin_gcp.resources.base.GraphBuilder.submit_work
documentation:
Use this method for work that can be done in parallel.
Example: fetching tags of a resource.

methods.name: fix_plugin_gcp.resources.base.GraphBuilder.node
documentation:
Returns first node on the graph that is of given `clazz`
and/or conforms to the `filter` and matches attributes given in `**node`.

methods.name: fix_plugin_gcp.resources.base.GraphBuilder.nodes
documentation:
Returns list of all nodes on the graph that are of given `clazz`
and/or conform to the `filter` and match attributes given in `**node`.

methods.name: fix_plugin_gcp.resources.base.GraphBuilder.add_edge
documentation: Creates edge between `from_node` and another node using `GraphBuilder.node(filter, **to_node)`.

methods.name: fix_plugin_gcp.resources.base.GraphBuilder.add_edges
documentation: Creates edges between `from_node` and all nodes found with `GraphBuilder.nodes(filter, **to_node)`.

class_variables.name: fix_plugin_gcp.resources.base.GcpMonitoringQuery.query_name
documentation: name of the metric (e.g., GCP metric type)

class_variables.name: fix_plugin_gcp.resources.base.GcpMonitoringQuery.stat
documentation: aggregation type, supports ALIGN_MEAN, ALIGN_MAX, ALIGN_MIN

methods.name: fix_plugin_gcp.resources.base.GcpResource.resource_raw_name
documentation:
Extracts the last segment of the GCP resource ID.

Returns:
    str: The last segment of the resource ID (e.g., "function-1" from "projects/{project}/locations/{location}/functions/function-1").

methods.name: fix_plugin_gcp.resources.base.GcpResource.adjust_from_api
documentation:
Hook method to adjust the resource before it is added to the graph.
Default: do not change the resource.

methods.name: fix_plugin_gcp.resources.base.GcpResource.post_process
documentation:
Hook method to post process the resource after it is added to the graph.
Default: do nothing.

methods.name: fix_plugin_gcp.resources.base.GcpResource.connect_in_graph
documentation:
Hook method which is called when all resources have been collected.
Connect the resource to other resources in the graph.

methods.name: fix_plugin_gcp.resources.base.GcpResource.post_process_instance
documentation:
Hook method to post process the resource after all connections are done.
Default: do nothing.

classes.name: fix_plugin_gcp.GCPCollectorPlugin

methods.name: fix_plugin_gcp.GCPCollectorPlugin.collect
documentation:
Run by fix during the global collect() run.

This method kicks off code that adds GCP resources to `self.graph`.
When collect() finishes the parent thread will take `self.graph` and merge
it with the global production graph.

methods.name: fix_plugin_gcp.GCPCollectorPlugin.collect_project
documentation:
Collects an individual project.

Is being called in collect() and either run within a thread or a spawned
process. Depending on whether `gcp.fork_process` was specified or not.

Because the spawned process does not inherit any of our memory or file
descriptors we are passing the already parsed `args` Namespace() to this
method.

methods.name: fix_plugin_gcp.GCPCollectorPlugin.add_config
documentation: Called by fix upon startup to populate the Config store

Google Cloud Platform fix collector plugin.

Gets instantiated in fix's Processor thread. The collect() method
is run during a resource collection loop.

functions.name: fix_plugin_gcp.collector.called_collect_apis
documentation: Return a list of all the APIs that are called by the collector during the collect cycle.

functions.name: fix_plugin_gcp.collector.called_mutator_apis
documentation: Return a list of all the APIs that are called to mutate resources.

methods.name: fix_plugin_gcp.resources.compute.GcpDiskType.post_process_instance
documentation: Adds edges from disk_types type to SKUs and determines ondemand pricing

classes.name: fix_plugin_gcp.resources.compute.GcpFirewallPolicy
documentation: TODO Firewall Policies are on org level, parentId is org id or folder id
